primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivables for designation as hedged items in a formally documented hedge accounting relationship in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Governs hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup transactions in consolidated statements, prohibiting hedging of transactions between group entities unless they involve external parties
Addresses the non-elimination of intragroup monetary items on consolidation and recognition of exchange differences in consolidated financial statements

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies that hedge accounting may only apply to foreign exchange differences between functional currencies of foreign operation and parent entity
Provides exception for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged item in consolidated statements when not fully eliminated on consolidation
Specifies recognition treatment for foreign exchange gains and losses on monetary items including those eligible for hedge accounting

peripheral_authority.reason: Disclosure requirements for hedge accounting and credit risk do not determine hedge eligibility
Provisions and contingencies framework not directly applicable to hedge accounting eligibility
Dividend measurement and recognition timing not relevant to hedge accounting designation

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup monetary items

approaches.label: Eligible hedged item for intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 para 6.3.6 provides an exception to the general prohibition on intragroup hedging, permitting the foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as a hedged item when exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation per IAS 21 para 45

approaches.label: Ineligible hedged item due to internal transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 para 6.3.5 requires hedged items to involve parties external to the reporting entity; dividend receivables from intragroup sources are internal transactions that do not meet this criterion for consolidated statement hedge accounting
